heroText: Brisbane is Queensland's river city: a warm-weather base for sport, culture, waterfront walks and trips deeper into eastern Australia.

highlights.name: Routes
highlights.text: Transit, day trips and event access.

history.label: Early settlement
history.text: The Brisbane region has been inhabited for more than 22,000 years by the Yagara (Yuggera), Turrbal and Quandamooka peoples.
The first outpost was founded at Redcliffe in 1824 before relocating to North Quay in 1825.

Brisbane grew as a river port serving pastoral districts and the Moreton Bay islands, while German and Scottish migrants established early agricultural settlements, notably the Zion Hill Mission at Nundah in 1838 and Fortitude.
The settlement closed in 1842, opening the district to free colonisation.

The Brisbane district became a major front of the War of Southern Queensland (1843–1855), involving coordinated resistance from Yuggera and Turrbal groups, with support from neighbouring Ningy Ningy people, under leaders such as Dundalli.
Brisbane became the capital of the newly separated Colony of Queensland in 1859.

history.label: 1860s
history.text: From the 1860s, Brisbane became a key Western Pacific port in the trade of blackbirded labour, serving as a major point of transit for South Sea Islander indentured labourers transported to plantation districts across.
The city suffered two destructive fires in 1864, which destroyed much of the early commercial centre and led to new building regulations favouring brick over timber construction.

history.label: 1890s
history.text: Vulnerability to natural hazards continued into the 1890s, when the Great Flood of 1893 inundated large areas of Brisbane, swept away the first Victoria Bridge and became one of the most significant floods in.

history.label: 20th century
history.text: Brisbane became the capital of the world's first labour movement government with the election of Anderson Dawson in 1899. 20th century Following Federation in 1901, Brisbane entered the new century as the capital of.
Severe floods such as the 1974 flood caused extensive damage and prompted major changes in water management, including the construction of Wivenhoe Dam.

history.label: 21st century
history.text: Following Expo 88, the South Brisbane riverfront was redeveloped into the South Bank Parklands, which opened in 1992 and became one of the city's major cultural precincts. 21st century The early 21st century has.

Major flood events in the 2011 and 2022 floods renewed focus on resilience and river management, while the severe rainfall associated with Cyclone Alfred in 2025 prompted further review of flood-mitigation planning.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: During the Second World War, Brisbane became a major Allied headquarters and logistics centre in the South West Pacific.

name: Brisbane
